raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX AT & T Universal Citicard texted me about a XXXX charge for {$15.00}. I contacted the company as directed to verify that charge was not mine. They closed the account & advised they would be sending another card. Citicard did not provide the account number to me but advised it is on the web account. 
On XX/XX/XXXX I signed onto my account and discovered two XXXX charges on XX/XX/XXXX : {$120.00} & {$75.00}. Immediately contacted the company to advise the charges were not mine. When the account was due I paid the valid charges on the account as advised by the customer service representative. 
On the XXXX statement these charges still appear & when I called, I was told that the customer service rep submitted it as a dispute instead of a fraudulent charge. XXXX responded to the dispute as a valid charge to my credit card number. ( At this point need to interject that I do NOT have a XXXX account ). 
When the bill arrived, I called & discussed at length with the cc department who then transferred me to Fraud Department then to the Security Operation department. Thought that all was understood and trusted that they would do as they outlined ... provide the credit to the account. 
The new card was not activated and NOT used. AT & T continued charging interest and late fees despite my continued calls & their assurance that corrections were in the works. This resulted in my credit rating change which distressed me so I made minimum payments to stop the harassing text messages and phone calls. 
At this point your intersection would be appreciated as I have called, written and been promised resolution. I have been a Citicard holder since XXXX with several accounts which are paid in full each month.
